description: SvelteKit frontend implementation for device management. All backend APIs exist and are tested. The dashboard uses SvelteKit 2 with Svelte 5 runes, Tailwind CSS 4 with OKLch dark theme tokens, lucide-svelte icons, and SvelteKit load functions. Existing framework-agnostic files are in place: src/lib/api/devices.ts (API client), src/lib/types/device.ts (TypeScript types), src/lib/constants/modbus-presets.ts (Modbus register presets). CRITICAL: All files MUST be .svelte or .ts — no .tsx, no React. Follow patterns from existing pages: sessions, profiles, settings.

userStories.title: Add Devices nav item to sidebar layout
userStories.description: As a user, I want a Devices link in the sidebar navigation so I can access device management from any page.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'Devices' nav item to the navItems array in src/routes/+layout.svelte
Uses lucide-svelte Cpu icon (import { Cpu } from 'lucide-svelte')
Links to '/devices' path
Placed between 'Profiles' and 'Settings' in the nav order
Active state uses the same amber highlight pattern as other nav items
npm run check passes with zero errors
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: The layout is at src/routes/+layout.svelte. It uses Svelte 5 runes ($props, $state). Icons are from lucide-svelte. Active detection: isActive(href) checks page.url.pathname.startsWith(href). Follow the exact same pattern as existing nav items.

userStories.title: Create device list page with discovered devices banner
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see all my configured devices in a card grid and any newly discovered pending devices in a highlighted banner so I can manage my roaster fleet.
acceptanceCriteria: New file src/routes/devices/+page.ts with SvelteKit load function that fetches all devices from the API using the framework fetch parameter
New file src/routes/devices/+page.svelte displays configured devices as a responsive card grid
Each device card shows: name, device_id, status badge, description (if set), location (if set), last_seen_at as relative time (e.g. '5m ago')

Status badges: active = green (bg-green-500/15 text-green-400), pending = amber (bg-amber-500/15 text-amber-400), disabled = gray (bg-muted text-muted-foreground), error = red (bg-red-500/15 text-red-400)
Pending/discovered devices shown in a highlighted banner at top with amber border, each with a 'Configure' button linking to the setup wizard
Empty state when no devices: centered message with Cpu icon suggesting to add a device or power on an MQTT device
'Add Device' button in page header links to /devices/new
'Device Profiles' button in page header links to /devices/profiles
Uses dark theme tokens (bg-card, text-foreground, border-border, etc.)
Uses lucide-svelte icons: Plus, Cpu, Signal, Wifi
npm run check passes with zero errors
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Use the API client from src/lib/api/devices.ts (import { listDevices } from '$lib/api/devices.js'). The load function pattern: export const load = async ({ fetch }) => { ... }. Follow the sessions/+page.ts pattern but call listDevices(). Card grid uses Tailwind: grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 xl:grid-cols-3 gap-4. Device cards should link to /devices/[id] via <a> tags. All styling must use the dark theme tokens from app.css (bg-card, text-foreground, border-border, text-muted-foreground, bg-accent for hover). IMPORTANT: The devices API client uses global fetch, so pass the SvelteKit fetch to it or call fetch directly in the load function.

userStories.title: Create multi-step device setup wizard
userStories.description: As a user, I want a guided step-by-step wizard to configure a new device so I can set up name, connections, and Modbus registers in a clear flow.
acceptanceCriteria: New file src/routes/devices/new/+page.svelte with a 4-step wizard

Step 1 (Basic Info): device_id (text input, required), name (text input, required), description (textarea, optional), location (text input, optional), profile_id (select dropdown populated from device profiles API)

Step 2 (Connections): Protocol selector (MQTT/WebSocket/Modbus TCP tabs), protocol-specific config fields: MQTT shows topic_prefix and QoS dropdown; WebSocket shows URL and reconnect_interval; Modbus TCP shows host, port, unit_id

Step 3 (Register Map, shown only if Modbus TCP selected): Modbus preset selector dropdown (from src/lib/constants/modbus-presets.ts), preview table of register entries, ability to add/remove/edit individual registers

Step 4 (Review & Test): Summary of all configuration, 'Test Connection' button that calls testConnection API and shows success/failure with latency, 'Create Device' button that submits the full config
Step indicator bar at top showing current step with completed/active/pending states
Back/Next buttons for navigation between steps, Next disabled until required fields filled
After successful creation, redirects to /devices/[id]
If URL has ?device_id= query param, pre-fills device_id field (for configuring discovered devices)
All inputs use dark theme styling: bg-input, border-border, text-foreground, focus:ring-amber-500
Uses lucide-svelte icons: ChevronLeft, ChevronRight, Check, Loader2, Wifi, Globe, Database
npm run check passes with zero errors
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Use Svelte 5 runes: let step = $state(1), let formData = $state({...}). For protocol-specific config, use a discriminated union pattern with $state. Use the API functions: createDevice from '$lib/api/devices.js', listDeviceProfiles, addConnection, setRegisterMap, testConnection. Import MODBUS_PRESETS from '$lib/constants/modbus-presets.js'. For navigation after create, use goto from '$app/navigation'. The wizard should be a single .svelte file managing steps with {#if step === 1} blocks. Form validation: check required fields are filled before allowing Next. Test connection button should show a spinner (Loader2 with animate-spin) while pending.

userStories.title: Create device detail and edit page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view and edit a configured device's details, connections, and register map so I can manage devices after initial setup.
acceptanceCriteria: New file src/routes/devices/[id]/+page.ts with load function that fetches device with connections via getDevice(params.id)
New file src/routes/devices/[id]/+page.svelte showing device detail view
Header section: device name, status badge, device_id, description, location, last_seen_at
Edit mode toggle: clicking 'Edit' button switches to inline edit mode for name, description, location, profile_id, status
Save/Cancel buttons in edit mode, calls updateDevice API
Connections section: lists all connections with protocol icon, enabled toggle, config summary
Add Connection button opens inline form with protocol selector and config fields
Each connection has Edit/Delete actions
Register Map section (shown if device has Modbus connection): table of registers with address, name, type, data_type, unit, writable columns
Delete device button with confirmation dialog, redirects to /devices after deletion
Test Connection button for each connection
Uses dark theme tokens throughout (bg-card, border-border, text-foreground, etc.)
Uses lucide-svelte icons: Pencil, Trash2, Plus, ToggleLeft/ToggleRight, Signal, Wifi, Database, ArrowLeft
Back button/link to /devices at top
npm run check passes with zero errors
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Load function pattern: export const load = async ({ params, fetch }) => { const res = await fetch('/api/devices/' + params.id); if (!res.ok) error(404, 'Device not found'); return { device: await res.json() }; }. Use $state for edit mode toggle and form data. For connection test, use testConnection from '$lib/api/devices.js'. Connection config display should show human-readable summaries: MQTT shows topic_prefix, WebSocket shows URL, Modbus shows host:port. Use goto('/devices') from '$app/navigation' after delete.

userStories.title: Create device profiles management page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create, view, and delete device profiles so I can reuse common device configurations across multiple devices.
acceptanceCriteria: New file src/routes/devices/profiles/+page.ts with load function fetching all device profiles
New file src/routes/devices/profiles/+page.svelte showing profile list
Profile list displayed as cards in a responsive grid, each showing: name, description, default_control_mode, default_setpoint, max_temp, telemetry_interval_ms
Create Profile button opens an inline form or modal with all profile fields

Profile fields: name (required), description, default_control_mode (manual/auto select), default_setpoint, default_fan_pwm, default_kp, default_ki, default_kd, max_temp, min_fan_pwm, telemetry_interval_ms
Delete button on each profile with confirmation
Back link to /devices
Empty state when no profiles: message suggesting to create one
Uses dark theme tokens throughout
Uses lucide-svelte icons: Plus, Trash2, ArrowLeft, Settings2
npm run check passes with zero errors
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Use listDeviceProfiles, createDeviceProfile, deleteDeviceProfile from '$lib/api/devices.js'. Pattern for inline create form: let showForm = $state(false), toggle with button click. After create/delete, re-fetch the list by invalidating or reloading. Use invalidateAll() from '$app/navigation' after mutations, or manage local state. Profile cards should show key values in a compact format: 'Setpoint: 200C, Mode: auto, Max: 240C'.

userStories.title: Add device selector dropdown to dashboard
userStories.description: As a user, I want to select which device to monitor on the main dashboard so I can switch between multiple configured roasters.
acceptanceCriteria: New component src/lib/components/DeviceSelector.svelte
Dropdown/select showing all active devices from the API
Displays device name and status indicator dot (green for active, amber for pending, red for error)
Emits selected device_id on change
Integrated into the dashboard page (+page.svelte) header area
Selected device is used to filter which device's telemetry to show (passed to WebSocket or used in API calls)
Falls back to first active device if none selected
Persists selected device in sessionStorage so selection survives page refreshes within the session
Uses dark theme styling matching other form controls
Uses lucide-svelte Monitor icon
npm run check passes with zero errors
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: The main dashboard is at src/routes/+page.svelte. The DeviceSelector should be a standalone component that other pages can import too. Use $state for the selected value. To fetch devices, call listDevices from '$lib/api/devices.js' in an onMount or use a small store. The telemetry WebSocket store at src/lib/stores/telemetry.ts currently connects without device filtering — the selector should pass the device_id to the store's init function if the store supports it, otherwise just display it for now. sessionStorage access: if (typeof window !== 'undefined') sessionStorage.getItem/setItem.
